info.title: Service Health Assurance Graph APIs
info.description: APIs for requesting assurance-graph data stored in the Crosswork Service Health application.

post.summary: Get impacted services list for a subservice.

200.description: OK. The request was successful. The result is contained in the response body.

post.summary: Get matching subservices for a service.

200.description: OK. The request was successful. The result is contained in the response body.

post.summary: Get service details for a service.

200.description: OK. The request was successful. The result is contained in the response body.

data.error: no service exists for the given Service ID

degradedPathsOnly.description: When a Crosswork user selects the check box in UI to show 'Down and Degraded only', the UI makes a query with this flag set to true. When set to true, the response will include only those subservices that are either in the down or degraded state, or those that are present in the path between the given service node and any of its subservice nodes that are in down or degraded state. When set to false, the response will include all the subservice nodes serving this given service instance.

graph_only.description: When set to true will return graph minimal data for consumers like UI to render VPN graph

ruleId.description: The rule used by the service.

profileId.description: The profile used by the service.

monitoringType.description: The monitoring type used for the service.

degradedPathsOnly.description: The response for down and degraded services. Refer to AssuranceReportRequest for details.

error.description: Error details in case the request failed.

rootSubservices.description: List of IDs of the subservice nodes that are directly connected to the Service node.

more_fragments.description: Flag to indicate if more Kafka message chunking fragments are available.

assuranceReportResponseStatus.description: Service assurance report is shared via NB using below schema.

componentError.description: Each component's list of error messages

componentErrorError.description: To store each component's list of error messages and its timestamp.

ErrorMaintenance.description: Represents a single error occurring in the computation and preventing its evaluation.

ErrorUninitialized.description: Presents a result error due to some initial unavailability of (sufficient) expression input data.

name.description: Name of this parameter. Examples: DEVICE, IFNAME, IFENTRY, VPN.

HPElemParamType.description: ParamType allows HPMgr to interpret the nature of the argument values passed while instantiating Heuristic Package elements like Rule, Subservice etc.

HealthStatus.description:
Service Health Statuses: 
 - UNKNOWN: Default state that CNC-ISTP displays when monitoring is not enabled for a given service.
  - MONITORING_INITIATED: When Service Health receives a monitoring request for a given service, the service gets initialized to this state.
  - MONITORING_FAILED: State reflecting that Service Health ran into some internal error or failure. Sample failures: collection job failure, unexpected feed coming in from device etc.
  - MONITORING_PAUSED: State reflecting that monitoring is in the 'pause' state; that is, monitoring is not active but historical data is available for inspection via the timeline.
 - SERVICE_UP: All the different subservices of the Service that are being monitored are healthy, with no issues to report.
 - SERVICE_DOWN: All the different subservices of the Service that are being monitored are reporting issues. Corresponding symptoms should provide more details about the issues being encountered.
 - SERVICE_DEGRADED: Some of the subservices of that Service that are being monitored are reporting issues. Corresponding symptoms should provide more details about the issues being encountered.

metricLabel.description: Metric identifier. SubserviceClass definition provides this label value.

unitType.description: Unit type for the given metric

updateTimestamp.description: Time when the feed was last updated

MonitoringStatus.description:
Monitoring statuses: 
 - MONITORING_UNKNOWN: default status when the monitoring status is unknown.
 - MONITORING_SUCCESS: Success state when there are no errors in monitoring.
 - ENCOUNTERED_ERRORS: State reflects the monitoring errors.

profileConstants.title:
map<key, value>
   key:    Constant name
   value:  Value defined for this constant in the respective Config Profile
Example:
  "profileConstants": {
       "CPU_THRESHOLD_MAX": "80"
  }

OutputSimpleValueStatus.description: Status which represents the health and symptoms.

OutputSimpleValueStringList.description: List of Output SimpleValue String List

OutputSimpleValueUintList.description: List of Output SimpleValue Uint List

subserviced.description: Request the services for a given subservice-id.

service_id.description: Request the subservices for a given subservice-id.

tags.description: To be used when Type is TAGGED_SUBSERVICES

RequestSubservicesType.description: Request for getting the list of Policy/Device subservices for a given service. (Supported types for MVP-1: TransportSubservices, DeviceSubservices)

services.description: There can be multiple services referring to a given subservice.

error.description: Error details in case of any error condition.

ResponseImpactedServicesServiceDetails.description: Providing the service details like service type and name along with ID.

ResponseImpactedServicesStatus.description: Response services details for a given subservice.

ResponseSubservicesSsStatus.description: Response subservices status details for a given subservice.

SoftRootSubserviceNodes.description: List of Subservice-ids that have a soft relationship with the service node.

args.description: List of arguments passed to this subservice.

values.description: Given argument could be a list of values like [Tor-A, Tor-B] for given key-name 'Devices'

healthTs.description: The last time the subservice health got updated

trackerTs.description: The last time the subservice health was updated due to health updates sent by the tracker.

ruleTriggerParamMap.description: Map to store the received conditionals parameters with their values.

paramMapWithTimestamp.description: Map with key as parameter name and value as timestamp.

staticId.description: Combination of subservice_class, namespace and config

dynamicSS.description: Flag indicating if the given subservice is dynamic

subDeps.description: Hard Dependencies: List of Subservice-ids which are of string type.

softSubDeps.description: Soft Dependencies: List of Subservice-ids which are of string type.

dynamicSubDeps.description: List of hard and soft dynamic subservice dependencies. These lists can change dynamically independent of the rest of the service graph.

dynamicSoftSubDeps.description: Dynamic Soft Dependencies: List of Subservice-ids which are of string type.

message.description: Represents a single error occurring in the computation and preventing its evaluation.

bearerAuth.description: Security token for authorizing requests for these APIs.
bearerAuth.name: Authorization
